heading: Short title; purpose; definition
label: 2000e–16a Short title; purpose; definition
notes: References in TextSections 2000e–16a to 2000e–16c of this title, referred to in text, was in the original “this title”, meaning title III of , which is classified generally to sections 2000e–16a to 2000e–16c of this title. For complete classification of title III to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 102–166

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Government Employee Rights Act of 1991, and not as part of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, title VII of which comprises this subchapter.Section was formerly classified to , The Congress.section 1201 of Title 2

Amendments1995— amended section generally, substituting “rights of certain government employees” for “right of Senate and other government employees” in subsec. (b) and striking out definitions of “Senate employee” and “head of employing office” in subsec. (c).Pub. L. 104–11994—Subsec. (c)(1)(B) to (D). , which directed the amendment of subsec. (B) and (C), was executed by making the amendment to subsec. (c)(1) to reflect the probable intent of Congress. Prior to amendment, subpar. (B) read as follows: “any employee of the Architect of the Capitol who is assigned to the Senate Restaurants or to the Superintendent of the Senate Office Buildings;”.Pub. L. 103–283

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: Sections 2000e–16a to 2000e–16c of this title may be cited as the “Government Employee Rights Act of 1991”.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: b Purpose
children.text: The purpose of sections 2000e–16a to 2000e–16c of this title is to provide procedures to protect the rights of certain government employees, with respect to their public employment, to be free of discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, or disability.

children.heading: “Violation” defined
children.label: c “Violation” defined
children.text: For purposes of sections 2000e–16a to 2000e–16c of this title, the term “violation” means a practice that violates .section 2000e–16b(a) of this title
